title_en: Methodology and Structure of Tadhkirat al-Huffaz
content_en:
Tadhkirat al-Huffaz — Memorial of the Hadith Masters — is adh-Dhahabi's biographical dictionary of the huffaz — the memorizers or masters of hadith, those scholars who had committed to memory a large corpus of hadiths and could evaluate their transmission with expertise. The work is a history of hadith scholarship from the Companions through adh-Dhahabi's own era, organized by generation (tabaqat), presenting the biographies of the leading authorities in each generation.

The concept of hafiz in the hadith tradition refers to a scholar of the highest level of mastery in the science of hadith — someone who has memorized tens of thousands of hadiths with their chains, understands the conditions of each narrator, and can evaluate the authenticity of transmissions with accuracy. Adh-Dhahabi sets strict criteria for inclusion: he includes only those scholars who were recognized by the tradition as having achieved this level of mastery, not merely those who knew a large number of hadiths.

Organized chronologically by generation, the work begins with the Companions who were the most prolific transmitters of hadith — figures like Aisha, Abu Hurayra, Abd Allah ibn Umar, and others. It then moves through the Successors, the generation after them, and the subsequent generations of hadith scholars, ending with adh-Dhahabi's own era. For each generation, he identifies the most important figures and provides biographical entries of varying length, depending on the importance and documentation of each scholar.

The entries typically include: the scholar's full name, genealogy, and place of origin; their teachers and students; an assessment of their level of mastery in hadith; evaluations of their reliability as a narrator and critic; and the date and place of their death. For the most important figures, the entries are extended accounts of their scholarly contributions and historical significance. For lesser-known figures, the entries may be brief.
content_id:
Tadzkirat al-Huffaz — Peringatan bagi Para Master Hadis — adalah kamus biografi al-Dzahabi tentang para huffaz — para penghafal atau master hadis, ulama-ulama yang telah menghafal korpus hadis yang besar dan dapat mengevaluasi transmisi mereka dengan keahlian. Karya ini adalah sejarah ilmu hadis dari para Sahabat hingga era al-Dzahabi sendiri, diorganisasi berdasarkan generasi (thabaqat), menyajikan biografi-biografi otoritas-otoritas terkemuka dalam setiap generasi.

Konsep hafiz dalam tradisi hadis merujuk pada seorang ulama dengan tingkat penguasaan tertinggi dalam ilmu hadis — seseorang yang telah menghafal puluhan ribu hadis beserta sanad-sanadnya, memahami kondisi setiap perawi, dan dapat mengevaluasi keaslian transmisi dengan akurat. Al-Dzahabi menetapkan kriteria ketat untuk dimasukkan: beliau hanya mencakup ulama-ulama yang diakui oleh tradisi sebagai telah mencapai tingkat penguasaan ini, bukan sekadar mereka yang mengetahui sejumlah besar hadis.

Diorganisasi secara kronologis berdasarkan generasi, karya ini dimulai dengan para Sahabat yang merupakan perawi-perawi hadis paling produktif — tokoh-tokoh seperti Aisyah, Abu Hurairah, 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar, dan lainnya. Kemudian bergerak melalui para Tabi'in, generasi setelah mereka, dan generasi-generasi berikutnya dari ulama-ulama hadis, berakhir dengan era al-Dzahabi sendiri. Untuk setiap generasi, beliau mengidentifikasi tokoh-tokoh paling penting dan menyediakan entri-entri biografi dengan panjang yang berbeda-beda, bergantung pada kepentingan dan dokumentasi setiap ulama.

Entri-entri biasanya mencakup: nama lengkap, silsilah, dan tempat asal ulama; guru-guru dan murid-muridnya; penilaian tentang tingkat penguasaannya dalam hadis; evaluasi-evaluasi tentang keandalannya sebagai perawi dan kritikus; serta tanggal dan tempat wafatnya. Untuk tokoh-tokoh paling penting, entri-entri merupakan uraian yang diperluas tentang kontribusi keilmuan dan signifikansi historis mereka. Untuk tokoh-tokoh yang kurang dikenal, entri-entri mungkin singkat. Struktur thabaqat ini memungkinkan pembaca untuk mengikuti perkembangan ilmu hadis secara berurutan dan melihat bagaimana setiap generasi belajar dari yang sebelumnya, menciptakan rantai transmisi yang tidak terputus dari masa Nabi hingga zaman al-Dzahabi.
content_ar: Tadhkirat al-Huffaz — Memorial of the الحديث Masters — is adh-Dhahabi's biographical dictionary of the huffaz — the memorizers or masters of الحديث, those العلماء who had committed to memory a large corpus of hadiths and could evaluate their transmission with expertise. The work is a history of الحديث scholarship from the الصحابة through adh-Dhahabi's own era, organized by generation (tabaqat), presenting the biographies of the leading authorities in each generation. The concept of hafiz in the الحديث التراث refers to a scholar of the highest level of mastery in the science of الحديث — someone who has memorized tens of thousands of hadiths with their chains, understands the conditions of each narrator, and can evaluate the authenticity of transmissions with accuracy. Adh-Dhahabi sets strict criteria for inclusion: he includes only those العلماء who were recognized by the التراث as having achieved this level of mastery, not merely those who knew a large number of hadiths. Organized chronologically by generation, the work begins with the الصحابة who were the most prolific transmitters of الحديث — figures like Aisha, Abu Hurayra, Abd الله ibn عمر, and others. It then moves through the التابعين, the generation بعد them, and the subsequent generations of الحديث العلماء, ending with adh-Dhahabi's own era. For each generation, he identifies the most المهم figures and provides biographical entries of varying length, depending on the importance and documentation of each scholar. The entries typically include: the scholar's full name, genealogy, and place of origin; their teachers and students; an assessment of their level of mastery in الحديث; evaluations of their reliability as a narrator and critic; and the date and place of their الموت. For the most المهم figures, the entries are extended accounts of their العلمي contributions and historical significance. For lesser-known figures, the entries may be brief.
